description: Conservative starter source spectral profile entries for Extension 2 exploratory mode. All entries are generic and research-flagged where appropriate. Do not interpret these as measured material performance.

entries.label: H200-Class Compute — Warm Band
entries.source_note: Wien-law derived from nominal die temperature range for H200-class devices. Cold-plate transport target ~300–330 K; die junction may reach 340–370 K. Emissivity basis is generic silicon/package estimate. Not a measured device spectrum.

entries.label: H200-Class Compute — Hotspot Band
entries.source_note: Represents hotspot zone within a high-TDP accelerator package. Peak junction temperature TJ,max = 120 C (393 K) is manufacturer maximum, not typical operating condition. Emissivity basis is conservative estimate for encapsulated silicon hotspot. Not a measured spectrum.

entries.label: Warm Transport Backbone Band
entries.source_note: Representative of warm-side transport fluid temperature range. Emissivity basis reflects generic fluid or transport surface. Not a measured spectrum of a specific fluid.

entries.label: Regenerative Hot Island Band
entries.source_note: Placeholder for a thermally elevated hot-island zone fed by staged spectral concentration. No hardware demonstration exists for this temperature range via waste-heat concentration from compute sources. Temperature basis is speculative target, not an achieved state. Treat as directional only.

entries.label: Solar-Polished Hot Island Band
entries.source_note: Highly speculative placeholder for a solar-concentration-augmented hot island in GEO orbit. GEO solar irradiance ~1361 W/m² is real; the achievable temperature with concentrator and this architecture combination is unvalidated. Do not treat as achievable performance. Research required before any engineering commitment.
